assessments.title: Architecture Work Runtime Capability Selection
assessments.confidence_reason: Direct implementation evidence found in orchestration flow steps and reasoning prompts.
assessments.rationale: The selection of independent workflows is fully implemented as evidenced by the existence of conversation_to_aw, discovery_to_aw, and vision_to_aw flow steps and their corresponding evolved prompts.

assessments.title: Governance Portal Implementation
assessments.confidence_reason: CI/CD workflow for portal publishing is active in the runtime snapshot.
assessments.rationale: The implementation of the governance portal is confirmed by the presence of the cas-portal-publish.yml workflow and the base_renderer.py in the rendering layer.

assessments.title: CAS Workflow Execution Model
assessments.confidence_reason: Core runner and engine logic reflect the standardized execution model.
assessments.rationale: The separation of orchestration (Runner) and reasoning (Engine/Workflows) is implemented in the core runtime modules and flow step logic.

assessments.title: Knowledge Provider Abstraction
assessments.confidence_reason: Explicit infrastructure and test evidence for the provider abstraction.
assessments.rationale: The Knowledge Provider abstraction is fully implemented and verified by runtime infrastructure changes and dedicated tests.

assessments.title: Architecture Work Registry as Authoritative Architectural Backlog
assessments.confidence_reason: Registry schemas and assessment logic are fully operational.
assessments.rationale: The Architecture Work Registry is now the authoritative source for governance, supported by specific JSON schemas and the architecture_work_assessment flow logic.

assessments.title: Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution
assessments.confidence_reason: Multiple components of the evolution plan are visible in the runtime snapshot.
assessments.rationale: Significant progress has been made on the governance action framework, compliance engine triggers, and portal evolution, though the full runtime-native loop is still maturing.
limitations: This is an oversized item covering multiple domains; coverage is substantial across the aggregate but incomplete for specific sub-goals.

assessments.title: Initial Discovery Flow and Alignment Baseline
assessments.confidence_reason: Discovery flow logic and baseline schemas are present.
assessments.rationale: The discovery-to-alignment lifecycle is operationalized through the discovery and discovery_to_aw flow steps and baseline assessment schemas.

assessments.title: Code Intelligence and Architecture Alignment
assessments.confidence_reason: Alignment engine and signal logic are present, but deep code structural analysis is emerging.
assessments.rationale: The foundation for code intelligence is established via the alignment engine and signal detection logic, though the 'full code understanding' vision is still in early implementation.

oversized_items.title: Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution
oversized_items.rationale: This item aggregates the Governance Portal, Action Framework, Compliance Engine, and Metrics into a single work item. These are independently deliverable and governed capabilities.

orphan_capabilities.description: Capability impact detected: Governance skill added: cas-drawio-generator
Capability impact detected: Enabled trace persistence
Capability impact detected: Enabled evaluation harness

obsolete_items.title: Architecture Knowledge Graph
obsolete_items.rationale: Duplicate of aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-002 (Architecture Knowledge Graph Development).

obsolete_items.title: Implement Automated CI Validation for CAS Skill Enforcement
obsolete_items.rationale: Duplicate of aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-05-31-005 (Automated CI Validation for CAS Skill Enforcement).

governance_health.overall_assessment: The CAS platform has successfully transitioned from a template-ready state to an operationally governed state for its core runtime and registry. Governance data quality is high for implemented items but shows redundancy in the backlog.
strengths: Strong alignment between core runtime implementation and Architecture Work intent.
Comprehensive semantic contract (JSON schema) coverage for governance artifacts.
Successful institutionalization of the Architecture Work Registry.
risks: Backlog redundancy (duplicate items for Knowledge Graph and CI validation).
Oversized evolution items may mask specific implementation gaps.
Orphan capabilities in telemetry and visualization suggest unrecorded architectural intent.

observations: Multiple duplicate AW items exist for Knowledge Graph, Business Intent, and CI Validation.
The 'Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution' item is too broad for precise lifecycle tracking.

recommendations.rationale: Redundancy in the registry creates confusion and potential for conflicting status updates.
recommendations.suggested_action: Merge aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-10 into aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-002 and retire the duplicate.

recommendations.rationale: Large items like 'Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution' are difficult to verify as 'Done'.
recommendations.suggested_action: Split aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 into separate items for Action Framework, Compliance Engine, and Metrics.

recommendations.recommendation: Map orphan capabilities to architectural intent.
recommendations.rationale: Telemetry and DrawIO generation are implemented but lack corresponding Architecture Work records.
recommendations.suggested_action: Create new Architecture Work items for Observability/Telemetry and Visualization capabilities.
